title: Group
description:
A Group is a work-unit / container template — the kind of organizational unit Roles are scoped within. Examples: BankDepartment, Hospital, Ward, Case, Workspace, Family, CourseOffering. Groups have attributes and lifecycle (a Case can be Opened, Settled, Closed); they're entity-like, but their organizational primary purpose is to be the container Roles are exercised in.

Group is one of four noun primitives (alongside Resource, Person, Role). All four share the noun base shape: name, optional attributes[], optional actions[], optional parent.

name.description: The singular PascalCase name of the Group template.

description.description: Human-readable explanation of what this Group represents.

domain.description: The dot-separated domain path this Group belongs to.

attributes.description: Fields that describe this Group template.

actions.description: Verbs this Group supports — lifecycle actions on the Group itself (Case.Open, Case.Settle, BankDepartment.Merge).

parent.description: Optional parent Group name for organizational hierarchy (Department.parent = Division; Ward.parent = Hospital).

examples.description: Representative example records for this Group template.

examples.name: Case
examples.description: A legal case grouping related parties, documents, and proceedings.

examples.name: BankDepartment
examples.description: An organizational unit within a bank.
